subject: Databases
title: Range Partitioning
description: A quick win: understand Range Partitioning.

hook.text: Partition by date: January data on node 1, February on node 2. Range query 'all of Q1' hits just three nodes.

buildup.text: Range partitioning keeps related data together. Queries on the partition key scan only one partition.

discovery.text: The risk: hot spots. If today's data is the busiest, one partition handles most traffic.

twist.text: Combine range with hash: partition by hash(user_id) first, then range by date within each partition.

climax.text: CockroachDB and Spanner use range partitioning. They automatically split and merge ranges as data grows.

punchline.text: Range partitioning: great for range queries, but watch for hot spots.

quiz.question: How can range partitioning create hot spots?
options: If most queries target recent data, the partition holding the latest range gets disproportionate traffic
Range partitioning always distributes data evenly
Hot spots only occur with hash partitioning
